title: Mosiera androsiana
intro: Mosiera androsiana, also known as Jamaican rosewood or West Indian rosewood, is a plant species belonging to the family Myrtaceae. The plant is valued for its aromatic, medicinal, and fragrant properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Bioactive constituents of Mosiera androsiana include essential oils, particularly limonene and alpha-pinene, as well as flavonoids like quercetin and kaempferol. Mosiera androsiana is commonly used in herbal preparations, including essential oils, teas, and infusions, which are often utilized in aromatherapy, traditional medicine, and natural remedies for various health conditions.
